title: Two Color TEM Labeling using Silver-Gold Intensification of DAB and CuDAB2
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"25b9v","text":"This method is use for two color labeling of cell organelles for transmission electro microscopy.  The color TEM labeling used Silver-Gold Intensification on polymerized DAB from photooxidation of miniSOG-mitomatrix and CuDAB2 labeling on DNA from oxidation of FeTAML-azide. The labeling were confirmed by EELS using a Gatan Imaging Filter on a ThermoFisher Talos 200C G2 transmission electron microscope at 200kV.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"d65cc","text":"The method is use for two color labeling of cell organelles for transmission electro microscopy.  80 um sections were collected and metals were verified using EELS from a Gatan Imaging Filter (GIF).  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Lipofectamine 3000 (Life Technologies)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"98rme","text":"17. standard GFPex475 filter set","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"27qak","text":"18. 1X PBS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: HEK293T cells are cultured on imaging plates containing poly-d-lysine coated glass bottom No. 0 coverslips (P35GC-0-14C, MatTek Corporation).

Cells are transiently transfected with miniSOG-mitomatrix fusion using Lipofectamine 3000 (Life Technologies). MiniSOG is fused to N-terminal of mitomatrix. Cells also are incubated with 10 μM EdU for 12hours.
Fix cells with 2% EM grade glutaraldehyde (18426, Ted Pella Incorporated) in 1XPBS, pH 7.2 (18851, Ted Pella Incorporated) containing for 5 minutes at 37 o C and then on ice for 55 minutes.
*Rinse once with fix after removing cell culture media. Afterwards add the fix for the total duration.
Blocking step for miniSOG: Block solution: (20 mM glycine, 10 mM KCN and 10 mM aminotriazole). Cells are blocked for 20 minutes on ice.
Wash 5X with 1X PBS buffer pH 7.2 for 2 minutes each on ice.

For mSOG, regions of interest are identified by standard GFPex475 filter set at light intensity of 10% and for photooxidation of DAB in 1X PBS at pH 7.2 the light intensity is set at 100%. A photooxidation reaction with DAB in 1X PBS at pH 7.2 until the desired brown intensity color from the precipitate for 2-5 minutes.
Wash 5X with 1X PBS, pH 7.2 for 2 minutes each on ice.
Wash 3X with 2% sodium acetate for 1 minutes each at room temperature.

Make enhancement solutions containing 3% hexamethylenetetramine in ddH 2 O, 5% silver nitrate in ddH 2 O, and 2.5% disodium tetraborate in ddH 2 O, mixed in a ratio of 20:1:2, prewarm solution for 10 minutes at 60 o C, added to the cells and incubated for 8-11 min at 60°C.
Wash 5X3 min with 2% sodium acetate at room temperature.
Incubate cells with 0.05% tetrachlorogold (III) acid trihydrate in ddH 2 O for 5 minutes at room temperature.
Wash 5X3 min with 2% sodium acetate at room temperature.
Incubated with 2.5% sodium thiosulphate in ddH 2 O for 4 min at room temperature.
Wash 1X3 min with 2% sodium acetate room temperature.
Wash 5X1 min with 1XPBS on ice.
Wash 2X with 1X PBS, pH 7.2 for 2 minutes each on ice.

The click reaction of the cells were carried out at room temperature and protected from light by using a mixture of 28 μM Fe-TAML azide solution, freshly prepared from 900 μl click buffer (100 mM NaCl 50 mM Na·HEPES pH 7.6), 10.0 μl CuSO4 (100 mM in water), 0.1% Saponin (Sigma, S-4521) and the reaction was initiated with 100 𝜇l of freshly prepared aqueous sodium ascorbate (100 mM).
After 30 minutes, a second 100 𝜇L aliquot of newly prepared aqueous sodium ascorbate (100 mM) was added for another 30 minutes incubation.
Cells are washed with PBS pH 7.4 (5 x 1 min) at room temperature.
Cells are washed with 100 mM NaCl 50 mM Na·Bicine pH 8.3 ( 5 x 1 min).

2 mM Cu-DAB2 solutions in 100 mM NaCl 50 mM bicine pH 8.3 were prepared similarly to previously reported solutions of Ln-DAB2 in cacodylate buffer (Adams et al 2016) by substituting with stock solutions of 0.1M CuSO4, 1M-bicine buffer pH 8.3 and 1M-NaCl.
10 μl of H 2 O 2 from a 30% stock solution was added to 10ml of the 2 mM Cu-DAB2.
The Cu-DAB2 reaction ran for one hour on the cells.
Cells are washed with 100 mM NaCl 50 mM Na·Bicine pH 8.3 ( 5 x 1 min).
Process for TEM.
Cells in MatTek plate are prewarmed at 60°C for 10 min in 2% sodium acetate.
creator: National Center for Microscopy and Imaging Research
